baseline_systems.fit_for_gal: hosted content moderation baseline for deep-review and product safety comparisons
baseline_systems.name: OpenAI Moderation API

baseline_systems.fit_for_gal: open multimodal safety-classifier baseline; useful for taxonomy and content-safety comparisons
baseline_systems.name: Meta Llama Guard 4

baseline_systems.fit_for_gal: enterprise guardrail and content-safety baseline with NeMo Guardrails integration
baseline_systems.name: NVIDIA Llama 3.1 NemoGuard Content Safety

baseline_systems.fit_for_gal: open moderation model and dataset reference for prompt, response, jailbreak, and refusal labels
baseline_systems.name: AllenAI WildGuard

baseline_systems.fit_for_gal: runtime security/product baseline for prompt injection, data leakage, and application-level defenses
baseline_systems.name: Lakera Guard

baseline_systems.fit_for_gal: vision safety reference for future multimodal lanes; not a direct v0 baseline
baseline_systems.name: Google ShieldGemma 2

benchmark_suites.fit_for_gal: primary benchmark family to build; measures application action governance, auditability, escalation, and latency
industry safety sanity benchmark for general chat-system risk; mostly applicable to deep-review lane
open moderation benchmark for prompt and response safety; useful for Phi-4 deep-review evaluation
agent harmfulness benchmark; useful for proposed-action governance and escalation evaluation
web-agent misuse benchmark; useful when GAL governs browser or web-action agents
agent safety-risk judgment benchmark; useful for deep-review and trace-review lanes
execution safety gate benchmark; close to GAL's authorization-conformance goal
robust refusal and red-team benchmark; useful for deep-review safety regression, not sidecar v0

gal_positioning.primary_claim: GAL is a low-latency advisory governance model for application actions and evidence, not a generic content moderation model.
